title: hieracium murorum subsp. subdidymocephalum
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. subdidymocephalum is a subspecies of the Hieracium murorum plant, commonly known as wall hawkweed, a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Hieracium murorum subsp. subdidymocephalum include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in water or oil, which can be used topically to apply its medicinal properties to the skin.
